NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM HAS CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO IN HANAHAN SOUTH CAROLINA. ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE 70 TO 85 MPH...WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 40 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH OF 0.25 MILES. THE TORNADO DEVELOPED OVER TURKEY CREEK AND TRACKED SOUTH-SOUTHWEST JUST EAST OF EAST RECESS ROAD AND TO THE WEST OF DENNIS DRIVE BEFORE DISSIPATING. STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS ALSO CAUSED EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE ALONG A 0.75 MILE WIDE AND 0.70 MILE LONG AREA ADJACENT TO THE TORNADO PATH.

AT LEAST ONE TREE DOWN ON A HOUSE. A TREE DOWN ON A VEHICLE. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ON POWERLINES. MOST OF THE DAMAGE IN THE NORTH MURRAY DRIVE VICINITY. SC ELECTRIC AND GAS REPORTING 3500 WITHOUT POWER MOSTLY IN THE HANAHAN AREA. NWS WILL LIKELY BE SENDING A STORM SURVEY TEAM TO THIS AREA SUNDAY MORNING.
